growth_records.medium: Pro99 (0.2-µm-filtered Sargasso Sea water + Pro99 nutrients), phosphate-limited
growth_records.cite: Kujawinski et al. Metabolite diversity among representatives of divergent Prochlorococcus ecotypes. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01261-22. [PMC10654061]
growth_records.snippet: we grew MIT9301 at the higher irradiance (50 µmol photons m-2 s-1) under semi-continuous phosphate limitation through daily transfers (at a dilution rate of 0.3 day-1) in media with 20-fold lower phosphate concentrations (i.e., 2.5 µM phosp
growth_records.notes: Phosphate-limited semi-continuous culture (daily transfers) at fixed dilution rate D=0.3 day^-1. At steady state mu=D=0.3 day^-1 = 0.3/24 = 0.0125 h^-1. td=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.0125=55.5 h (=ln2/0.3=2.31 day). culture_mode set to chemostat as closest strict match to fixed-dilution-rate steady state (verbatim 'semi-continuous ... daily transfers' in culture_detail). No uptake/secretion fluxes reported (metabolomics gives intracellular/extracellular pool concentrations in attomoles/cell, not fluxes). Full Pro99 recipe in cited ref 85, not in text (only phosphate 2.5 µM P-limited vs 50 µM replete given); medium_composition_stated=false.
